This is the 3000kg model in a family of large high-explosive bombs, and is primarily to be used against hard targets such as large warships, bunkers, or airbases.

wp_fab-9000_m54.Name: FAB-9000 M-54
wp_fab-9000_m54.Description: The FAB-1500 is a Soviet-designed 9000 kg (20,000 lb) 'general purpose' air-dropped bomb with a high-explosive warhead primarily used by the Soviet Union and its allies. This is the 9000kg model in a family of large high-explosive bombs, and is one of the largest conventional bombs ever used in combat, with a focus on obliterating hardened targets.

wp_kab-500l.Name: KAB-500L
wp_kab-500l.Description: The KAB-500L is a Soviet-designed 500 kg (1100 lb) laser-homing air-dropped bomb with a high-explosive warhead primarily used by the Soviet Union and its allies. This is based off the FAB-500, but with a laser seeker and fins for guidance.

wp_rbk-250.Name: RBK-250
wp_rbk-250.Description: The RBK-250 is a Soviet 250 kg (550 lb) cluster bomb.

wp_rbk-500.Name: RBK-500
wp_rbk-500.Description: The RBK-500 is a Soviet 500 kg (1100 lb) cluster bomb.

wp_plab-100.Name: PLAB-100
wp_plab-100.Description: The PLAB-100 is Soviet-designed 100 kg (220 lb) anti submarine air-dropped bomb primarily used by the Soviet Union and its allies.

plan_skf-1.Name: SKF-1
plan_skf-1.Description: The SKF-1 is a passive-only omnidirectional sonobuoy used by the People's Liberation Army Navy since 1960s.

plan_skf-2.Name: SKF-2
plan_skf-2.Description: The SKF-2 is a passive, directional sonobuoy used by the People's Liberation Army Navy since mid-1970s.

usn_ssq-41.Name: AN/SSQ-41
usn_ssq-41.Description: The SSQ-41A "Jezebel" is a Low Frequency Analysis and Recording (LOFAR) type sonobouy. This is the most common NATO passive sonobuoy, and has no ability to determine bearing like the later DIFAR sonobuoy, but still has decent sensitivity, and can be found in large numbers in any Western navy's stockpile.

usn_ssq-47.Name: AN/SSQ-47
usn_ssq-47.Description: The SSQ-47A "Julie" is a continuously active range-only type sonobouy. This is the most common NATO active sonobuoy, and has no ability to determine bearing like the later DICASS sonobuoy, but is still useful to 'fix' a previously detected target with multiple buoys. As a result of the continuous active pinging, this sonobuoy has a lifespan of only 30 minutes.

usn_ssq-53.Name: AN/SSQ-53
usn_ssq-53.Description: The SSQ-53 is a Directional Frequency Analysis and Recording (DIFAR) type sonobouy. This gives the sonobuoy operator magnetic heading information which is a huge improvement over the earlier omnidirectional CRT-1.

usn_ssq-62.Name: AN/SSQ-62
usn_ssq-62.Description: The AN/SSQ-62 DICASS is an active sonobouy used for fixing a submerged submarine that has previously been located with the SSQ-53 passive sonobouys.

usn_ssq-77.Name: AN/SSQ-77
usn_ssq-77.Description: The SSQ-77A is a VLAD type (Vertical Line Array DIFAR) passive sonobuoy, designed to be a generational improvement for the original DIFAR type passive sonobuoy. This buoy has a similar architecture to the DIFAR buoy, but incorporates an extremely sensitive sonar array that allows for extremely long passive range for a sonobuoy, high capability against quiet, deep-diving targets and exhibits good performance operating in cluttered, noisy waters.\n\nThe consequence of this greater performance is the weight and cost of this buoy, meaning these are less common, and their accuracy at range is poor, so they are mostly useful for localization of submerged targets.

usn_ssq-101.Name: AN/SSQ-101
usn_ssq-101.Description: The AN/SSQ-101 Air Deployed Active Receiver (ADAR) is a sonobuoy designed as a receiver for shallow-water multi-static systems. When deployed it uses a large array of hydrophones to determine accurate bearings.

wp_rgb-1.Name: RGB-1
wp_rgb-1.Description: The RGB-1 is a passive-only omnidirectional sonobuoy used by the Soviet Union.

wp_rgb-nm1.Name: RGB-NM-1
wp_rgb-nm1.Description: The RGB-NM-1 is a passive-only omnidirectional sonobuoy used as part of the Baku suite by the Soviet Union.

wp_rgb-3.Name: RGB-3
wp_rgb-3.Description: The RGB-3 is an active-only sonobuoy used by the Soviet Union.

usn_zuni.Name: Zuni
usn_zuni.Description: The Zuni is a 5.0 in Folding-Fin Aircraft Rocket (FFAR) primarily carried by ground attack aircraft and attack helicopters. The Zuni can be used to carry various types of warheads similar to other FFAR systems. It is usually fired from the LAU-10 rocket pod holding four rockets.

usn_hydra.Name: Hydra 70
usn_hydra.Description: The Hydra 70 is a 2.75-inch Folding-Fin Aircraft Rocket (FFAR) used by both fighters and helicopters. The Hydra is capable of being fitted with a range of warheads and is normally carried in pods of up to 19.

wp_s-5.Name: S-5
wp_s-5.Description: The S-5 is a Soviet 55 mm Folding-Fin Aircraft Rocket (FFAR) used by fighter bombers and helicopters. The S-5 can trace its heritage to the German R4M of the late WWII era. Th rocket is normally carried in pods of up to 32.

wp_s-8.Name: S-8
wp_s-8.Description: The S-8 is a Soviet 80 mm Folding-Fin Aircraft Rocket (FFAR) used by fighter bombers and helicopters. The S-8 has a number of different variants including specialist runway denial variants. The rocket is normally carried in pods of up to 20.

wp_s-24.Name: S-24
wp_s-24.Description: The S-24 is a Soviet 240 mm Folding-Fin Aircraft Rocket (FFAR) used by fighter bombers and helicopters. Unlike most of FFAR it is carried singularly.

wp_s-25.Name: S-25
wp_s-25.Description: The S-25 is a Soviet 266 mm Folding-Fin Aircraft Rocket (FFAR) used by fighter bombers and helicopters. The largest of the Soviet Union's unguided rockets the S-25 has a warhead containing nearly 60kg of explosives and is carried singularly.

ir_107mm_rocket.Name: Haseb-1
ir_107mm_rocket.Description: The Haseb-1 rocket is an Iranian version of the Chinese 107mm rocket fired by the Type 63 Multiple Rocket Launcher. In Iranian service both the launcher and the rocket are called Haseb.

usa_m26.Name: M26
usa_m26.Description: The M26 is a 227 mm unguided rocket used by the U.S. Army's M270 Multiple Launch Rocket System (MLRS). The rocket payload consists of up to 800 submunitions able to cover up to 2000 square meters.

wp_grad.Name: Grad
wp_grad.Description: The M-21OF Rocket is a Soviet weapon fired from the BM-21 launch vehicle. The complete system (the M-21) is more commonly known as the "Grad" Multiple Launched Rocket System.

rn_type_182_decoy.Name: Type 182
rn_type_182_decoy.Description: The Type 182 towed countermeasure is a British torpedo decoy used by the Royal Navy on surface vessels. This decoy can emit false sonar pulses to jam incoming torpedo seekers, so long as the decoy is between the torpedo and the ship.

usn_an_slq_25_nixie_decoy.Name: AN/SLQ-25
usn_an_slq_25_nixie_decoy.Description: The Nixie (formally the AN/SLQ-25) is a towed decoy system utilised primarily by the surface ships of the USN. The System generates signals designed to lure approaching torpedoes away from the host platform.

usn_moss.Name: MOSS
usn_moss.Description: The Mk 70 MOSS (Mobile Submarine Simulator) is a 10 inch diameter decoy used to decoy enemy sonar away from the launching submarine. The MOSS is capable of simulating the self noise of a submarine as well as its active sonar.

wp_mg-44.Name: MG-44
wp_mg-44.Description: The MG-44 Korund is a second generation self-propelled hydro-acoustic submarine simulator. It is designed to be launched from special 400mm tubes on Soviet submarines.

wp_mg-74.Name: MG-74
wp_mg-74.Description: The MG-74 Korund-2 is a third generation self-propelled hydro-acoustic submarine simulator. Unlike the earlier MG-44, it can be launched from standard 533mm tubes, and includes highly sophisticated noise making, deceptive emitting, or acoustic jamming capabilities, and can also be deployed from aircraft.

wp_dc_shell.Name: BB-1 depth charge
wp_dc_shell.Description: BB-1 soviet depth charge for use against enemy submarines.

pla_depth_charge.Name: Depth Charge
pla_depth_charge.Description: Aircraft-dropped depth charge, 150kg mass, for use against enemy submarines.
